verification_notes: Founded 1889 by Alexander Broussard. Five generations. Recent transition from 4th-gen Jim Broussard to 5th-gen Blue Broussard III and Jayme Broussard Toeppich. Six locations across SE TX.
owner_name: Blue Broussard III; Jayme Broussard Toeppich (5th gen)

signals.evidence: Jim Broussard (4th gen) announced transfer to 5th gen Blue Broussard III and Jayme Broussard Toeppich. Jayme appointed CEO; she completed a 2016 Rice University Graduate Certificate in Leadership (suggesting ~2010s college grad, ~30-40s today). Succession just completed — owners not aging out for ~20-30 years.
Six locations (Beaumont x2, Kountze, Nederland, Silsbee, Winnie) plus on-site crematory. Larger than typical solo-owner target.
Modern website, fresh obituaries (May 2026), uses Passare CRM platform per Passare testimonial page. Crematorium added recently. Active leadership transition.
Website explicitly distinguishes from 'corporate funeral home buyouts and takeovers' — but with young new owners just installed, this is generational commitment not sale signal.

enrichment_notes: DECLINE: 5th-gen succession just completed; new owners (Jayme + Blue III) are young, well-trained, actively expanding. This is a strong family business but NOT a likely seller in 12-36 months. Place on long watch.

verification_notes: Founded 1946 by Ambrose and Lannie Claybar. 3 generations active: Brown Claybar (Chairman, 2nd gen) and Warren Claybar (President, 3rd gen, elected May 2007). Operates 3 funeral home locations + 5 cemeteries + crematory + florist.
owner_name: Warren Claybar (President); Brown Claybar (Chairman of the Board)

signals.evidence: Brown Claybar (Chairman) was 59 in 2011 per local news article = approx 74-75 in 2026. Warren Claybar (President, 3rd gen) elected to lead in May 2007 — likely 50s-60s now and runs operations day-to-day.
Brown Claybar served 9 years as Orange Mayor + city council + school board — heavy civic involvement = social capital, ties to community = succession likely intra-family (Warren), not external sale.
Vertically integrated: 3 funeral home locations, 5 cemeteries, crematory, full-service florist (Blooms by Trendz). Larger than typical solo-owner target — possibly $5M+ revenue.
New state-of-the-art crematory completed 2022. Modern website with online obituaries. Roughly 19 staff members listed. Not coasting.
Warren already President since 2007 (3rd gen); Brown still Chairman; succession partially complete. If Warren has children entering business this would be 4-gen.

enrichment_notes: B-TIER WATCH: Warren (3rd gen) is the operational owner — likely 50s-60s, succession in active phase. Brown (2nd gen, 75) as Chairman could trigger sale on his retirement, but more likely succession to 4th gen if available. Vertically integrated business is larger than typical ETA target. Watch for Warren retirement signals or generational gap.

verification_notes: REJECT: Legacy Funeral Group acquired Combest Family Funeral Homes from Kevin and Tonya Combest in May 2022. Independence claim in original spine was INCORRECT — needs correction.

signals.evidence: Acquired by Legacy Funeral Group in May 2022. Legacy is a multi-state funeral consolidator with operations in 8 states. NOT independent any longer.

enrichment_notes: EXCLUDE — CHAIN OWNED. Acquired by Legacy Funeral Group May 2022. Owners already sold. Spine record was stale. Add Legacy Funeral Group to exclusion list (alongside SCI/Dignity/Carriage etc.).

verification_notes: Founded 1989 by Joe F. Ramos and Rudy C. Robles. 4 El Paso locations (East 1989, Northeast 1996, West 2001, Americas newer). Current owners: Joe Ramos and Belinda Ramos. Rudy Robles Jr. (2nd gen) and Michael Ramos (likely 2nd gen) on staff. Independent — no chain affiliation found. 58+ staff per website.

4 locations + on-site crematory + 58+ staff + self-describes 'one of fastest growing funeral firms in Texas'. Multi-location chain in El Paso. Probably $7M-$15M revenue. UPPER edge of independent operator size.

If 2nd gen sons decline succession, this becomes a 2026-2028 sale. Watch for changes in leadership listings on website.

verification_notes: Founded 1924 by Howard and Paul Boxwell. 4 generations: 1st Howard+Paul (1920s); 2nd Ivan; 3rd Jim (d. Aug 2024 age 85) and Mike Boxwell (now sole 2nd gen survivor); 4th Brad (joined 1998) and Bart (joined 1991), Mike's sons; 5th Blakely Boxwell Stephens joined 2018. 6 Panhandle locations.

Brad joined 1998 (28 years), Bart joined 1991 (35 years). Both are seasoned 4th-gen funeral directors. Blakely (5th gen) onboarded 2018. Strong intra-family succession in place = sale less likely. Brad/Bart are themselves likely 50-60.
6 locations across Panhandle (Amarillo, Dumas, Vega, Panhandle, Claude, Stratford). Likely $4M-$8M revenue. Above solo-owner ETA threshold.

enrichment_notes: DECLINE — strong intra-family succession to 4th+5th gen. Watch list only if Brad/Bart unexpectedly retire early or Blakely transitions out.

verification_notes: Founded late 1952. 5 generations: 3rd gen Raymond Callaway Jones (deceased 2009); 4th gen Mike Jones (born 1956, deceased 2004 at age 48); 5th gen Cody D. Jones (owner, son of Mike) + mother Lorene A. Jones. Also operates Cozart Funeral Home and Restever Memorial Park Cemetery.

legal_name: Thompson's Harveson & Cole Funeral Homes & Crematory
verification_notes: Founded 1911 by Quincy Adams Harveson. Guy Thompson + Kathleen bought 1981 (3rd gen). Daughter Lucinda (Cindy) Thompson ran it; Martin Thompson (Lucinda's brother) acquired April 2020 as 'keeping it in family.' Currently led by Martin Thompson, wife Janice Thompson, son Jon Thompson (4th gen).
owner_name: Martin Thompson; Janice Thompson; Jon Thompson (son, 4th gen)

signals.evidence: Martin Thompson worked at father Guy's funeral home 22 years before opening Grapevine in 1998 = started 1976. If at 22 in 1976, born ~1954, now ~72. RIGHT IN THE AGING-OUT WINDOW.
Jon Thompson (Martin's son, 4th gen) actively in business. Intra-family succession candidate already in place.

Martin Thompson acquired sister Cindy's Thompson's Harveson & Cole April 2020. Consolidated under combined entity Thompsons Cremations & Funeral Services. Family-owned, but business is undergoing transformation.
Operates Thompson's Harveson & Cole + Martin Thompson & Son Funeral Home. Multi-location, 100+ year old business. Likely $4M-$8M revenue, above solo-owner target.

Self-describes as 'one of the few remaining family-owned and managed funeral homes in the Dallas/Fort Worth metroplex' — minority in heavily-consolidated market. If Jon Thompson does not continue, this is a likely future sale.

enrichment_notes: A-TIER WATCH: Martin (~72) in aging-out window with son Jon (4th gen) in business. Three potential outcomes: (1) Jon takes over (most likely given 4 gens), (2) sale to consolidator if Jon declines, (3) hybrid. Multi-entity scale above ETA target. Watch for Jon's role expansion signals or external sale signals in next 24-36 months.
